title: aglaia unifolia
intro: Aglaia unifolia, also known as the Malay apple or yellow aglaia, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The seeds, leaves, and bark of the aglaia unifolia plant have been used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for use in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
